fields.description:
# Code-review near-term nit reduction + new-behavior-without-test landmine detector

## Why
Code-review advisory volume is ~5 findings/review, dominated by docs + llm-prompts nits (0/9 high-value in
adjudication), while the highest-value landmine - new behavior shipped with no test - is buried by the old
scalar (0.415). We want less nit friction and a reliable catch for the landmine class.

## What
- Down-rank/suppress the `docs` and `llm-prompts` advisory surfaces and the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-
  landed-sibling' pattern that fired repeatedly as false-major nits (concretely: lower those criteria's
  effective priority / advisory-cap contribution in `code_review/criteria_routing.json`).
- Add a 'new-behavior-without-test' detector, shipped advisory/permissive first (the most objective landmine
  class).

## Detector mechanism (grounded; feasibility)
A purely textual DET cannot reliably identify 'new public behavior', so this is a GROUNDING-ASSISTED
heuristic detector (fail-open on ambiguity), built on rebar's existing code-grounding oracle backends
(ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md - already used by the AGENT-tier criteria):
- ADDED-SYMBOL SET: from the diff's added lines, use the grounding backend to extract newly-added
  EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions (language-aware; not a regex).
- TEST-CORRELATION: check whether the same change adds or modifies a test that references any added symbol
  (test-file heuristic: paths matching the repo's test globs; symbol-name reference).
- FIRE: an added exported symbol with NO correlated test change -> advisory 'new-behavior-without-test'
  finding. Ambiguous/unparseable diff -> ABSTAIN (fail-open, never blocks). It is deliberately advisory/
  permissive at rollout; only after the code-review impact redesign + calibration would blocking be
  considered.
- PLUG-IN: follows the existing conflict-marker DET pattern in `code_review/detectors.py` (registered like
  the other DET detectors); coaching surfaced through the existing `code_review_coach` path.
- BACKEND FALLBACK: if no grounding backend is available (none installed) OR the diff/patch cannot be parsed
  into files+added-lines, the detector ABSTAINS (returns no finding) — never raises, never blocks.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing), the new detector
  in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py` (reusing the grounding backend), and the coach surfacing. No
  LLM-priority-threshold blocking here (that depends on the code-review impact redesign child).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `docs` + `llm-prompts` advisory surfaces are down-ranked/suppressed in
      `code_review/criteria_routing.json`, asserted by a unit test that the packaged routing marks those
      criteria non-blocking / down-ranked (and that a docs-only advisory no longer surfaces above the cap).
      Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py -v`. (The ~nit-volume drop
      on the analysis sample diffs is illustrative/offline, NOT gated here.)
- [ ] A grounding-assisted `new_behavior_without_test` detector exists in `code_review/detectors.py` using
      the ast-grep/ctags grounding backend to find added exported symbols and correlate test changes;
      registered like the existing conflict-marker DET; advisory/permissive; ABSTAINS (fail-open, returns no
      finding, never raises) on an unparseable/ambiguous diff OR when no grounding backend is available.
- [ ] Regression eval cases in `tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py` (all green): (a) MUST-FIRE on a
      diff adding an exported symbol with no covering test; (b) MUST-NOT-FIRE on a diff that adds/updates a
      test referencing the added symbol; (c) ABSTAINS on a docs-only diff; (d) ABSTAINS on an
      unparseable/garbled diff. The detector's finding is advisory (never blocking) at this rollout.
